title: Varnish Backend Connection Reuse Signals
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Connect for Syslog, or HTTP Event Collector; `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for listed sourcetypes
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="varnish:stats"
| eval conn=tonumber(backend_conn), reuse=tonumber(backend_recycle)
| eval reuse_ratio=if(conn>0, round(100*reuse/conn,1), null())
| timechart span=15m avg(reuse_ratio) by host
description: Poor reuse increases latency and CPU on both sides.
value: Guides keep-alive and TLS session settings.
implementation: Low reuse may indicate TLS handshake storms toward origin.
visualization: Time series for rates and latency, top/dedup for hotspots, single-value alerts on health thresholds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch varnish backend connection reuse signals and catch issues early, before they turn into outages for the people who rely on the network.
knownFalsePositives: Edge cases: multi-vendor mixes and ad hoc feeds can include lab traffic and partial visibility that mimics issues until scopes are defined. Tuning tip: match this to «Varnish Backend Connection Reuse Signals» and exclude change windows, scans, and lab VLANs you already expect.
